Spain’s HealthTech startup Tucuvi raises €17 million to scale LOLA voice AI, reporting up to 80% automation in nursing follow-up
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
Tucuvi, a Madrid-based HealthTech company innovating certified voice AI, raised €17 million ($20 million) in Series A funding to accelerate the adoption of its AI-based care management platform in healthcare systems.
The round was led by Cathay Innovation and Kfund, through its early-growth fund Leadwind, with participation from existing investors Frontline Ventures, Seaya Ventures, and Shilling.
“Healthcare is under enormous pressure, and partial solutions are no longer enough,” explains María González, CEO of Tucuvi. “To continue providing quality care at scale, healthcare systems need AI they can trust: secure, auditable, and designed for real clinical environments. We are proud to already be working with more than 60 healthcare organisations and continue to grow to meet increasing demand.” (Translated).
Tucuvi’s Series A round can be situated within a wider, still relatively selective flow of European capital into AI-driven healthcare platforms during 2025–2026.
In early 2025, Warsaw-based primary care startup Jutro Medical raised €12 million in a Series A round to expand its network of physical clinics and deploy medical AI agents across telemedicine and operational workflows. This was followed later in the year by a €24 million Series A extension, also reported by EU-Startups, to further scale its AI-first primary care model and automation capabilities across clinical and administrative functions.
Against this backdrop, Tucuvi’s Madrid-based focus on certified voice AI and end-to-end care management workflows reflects a parallel approach to addressing workforce shortages and care complexity, albeit through software deployed directly into existing healthcare systems rather than through direct care delivery.
Taken together, the roughly €53 million raised across these announcements points to a growing, though still concentrated, level of investment in European healthcare AI companies seeking to increase care capacity and operational efficiency through automation.
“The Tucuvi platform transforms protocols and clinical guidelines into real, reliable, and consistent AI workflows, allowing us to interact with all our patients and enabling our nursing team to devote their expertise to cases that require high-value clinical judgment,”  adds an unidentified Medical Director. (Translated).
Healthcare systems are facing increasing pressure from a shortage of professionals, rising demand for care, and greater operational complexity. Tucuvi was founded in 2019 to respond to this context with secure, auditable, and autonomous AI agents that aim to expand care capacity without compromising clinical quality or trust.
The company has developed an AI-powered Care Management platform designed to automate and orchestrate high-volume care workflows from start to finish.
The platform is based on AI agents, including LOLA, a voice-based AI agent that conducts secure phone conversations with patients, executes clinical and care coordination workflows, and escalates to human teams when necessary.
Tucuvi currently supports more than 50 workflows, ranging from post-surgical follow-up and discharge to chronic disease management, preoperative assessments, test preparation, screening programs, appointment scheduling, and demand management.
Tucuvi’s voice AI agents handle phone interactions, while the platform as a whole takes care of workflow logic, referrals, documentation, and integration with existing healthcare system operations.
“What convinced us at Cathay Innovation is Tucuvi’s ability to combine clinical rigor, regulatory credibility, and real-world scalability in a single platform,” said Jacky Abitbol, Managing Partner at Cathay Innovation. “Its approach allows healthcare organisations to deploy autonomous AI with confidence, freeing up significant capacity for care teams while maintaining the highest levels of safety and quality. We believe Tucuvi is blazing a new trail in how AI can be responsibly integrated into healthcare operations.”
Tucuvi is reportedly the first AI platform to obtain European regulatory approval as a Class IIb Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) for both its voice agent and patient management platform.
The company outlines that their platform has generated consistent and measurable results in collaborating healthcare systems, including:
 Up to 80% automation of nursing follow-up flows
 Patient reach and adherence greater than 90%, even in highly complex populations
 Improved outcomes in chronic diseases, such as a 5.5% reduction in readmissions for COPD
With this funding, Tucuvi will accelerate its growth strategy in Europe and the United States, expand its AI capabilities, and continue to evolve its platform as a comprehensive action and intelligence system for care teams.

What are the use cases of Mews?
1. Use Cases by Category Front‑Desk Automation: Streamlines check‑in, check‑out and housekeeping communication. Self‑service kiosks reduce check‑in time by 5 minutes. Digital key issuance eliminates front‑desk bottlenecks. Automated housekeeping notifications cut call volume. Revenue Management & Upselling: Optimizes pricing and drives ancillary sales. Dynamic pricing engine (Atomize) boosts RevPAR by 39%. Embedded upsell offers increase average RevPAG by 70%. Direct‑booking engine lifts bookings by 45%. Guest Experience Personalisation: Creates seamless, end‑to‑end journeys. Mews Guest Journey platform unifies booking, check‑in, in‑stay services. Online check‑in/out and mobile payments cut guest wait times. Smart‑device integrations (e.g., Operto) enable contact‑free stays. Multi‑Property & Chain Management: Centralises operations across dozens of locations. Single dashboard controls 12,500+ properties worldwide. Unified reporting provides real‑time KPI visibility. Marketplace of 800+ third‑party apps scales functionality. Integrations & Marketplace: Extends core PMS with specialist tools. Open API enables instant connection to accounting, AI, and distribution systems. Marketplace offers plug‑and‑play modules for loyalty, marketing, and POS. Seamless payment processing automates 100% of transactions. 2. Scenarios and Narratives Rapid On‑boarding of a Boutique Aparthotel: The property replaces a legacy system with Mews, achieving a two‑week remote deployment and 100% online onboarding. Scenario: The hotel’s small IT team uses Mews Implementation Manager’s guided rollout, enabling staff to learn the interface in days. Guests now self‑check‑in via mobile, saving five minutes per arrival, while 60% of payments are processed automatically, freeing up admin time. Chain‑wide Revenue Optimisation: A hotel group leverages the Atomize RMS to adjust rates in real time across 30 locations. Scenario: By feeding market data into the cloud‑native engine, the chain sees a 39% uplift in RevPAR and a 70% increase in average RevPAG through targeted upsells, all visible in a single analytics dashboard. Seamless Guest Journey for a Hostels Network: Hosts integrate Mews with smart‑lock and IoT platforms. Scenario: Guests book online, receive a digital key on their phone, and can request housekeeping via the virtual concierge. The integration reduces front‑desk calls and improves NPS, while the marketplace supplies a one‑click loyalty app. 3. Strategic Benefits Operational Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks cuts staff workload, shortens check‑in times by five minutes and eliminates manual billing errors. Revenue Growth: Direct‑booking engine, dynamic pricing, and automated upsells deliver double‑digit percentage lifts in bookings and RevPAR. Scalability & Agility: Cloud‑native architecture supports rapid rollout to new properties without downtime, enabling multi‑property expansion. Guest Loyalty & Satisfaction: Unified guest journey and contact‑free services increase satisfaction scores and repeat bookings. 4. Core Functionalities Property Management System (PMS): Centralised reservation, billing, and housekeeping module with real‑time status updates. Revenue Management System (RMS – Atomize): AI‑driven pricing optimisation that reacts to market demand instantly. Booking Engine & Digital Key: Integrated front‑end for direct bookings and mobile key issuance. Mews Marketplace & Open API: Over 800 pre‑built integrations and a developer‑friendly API for custom extensions. Embedded Payments: PCI‑DSS‑compliant payment processing that automates 100% of transactions. 5. Customer Success Examples Locale (US aparthotel brand): Achieved 100% online onboarding, saved five minutes per check‑in, and drove >50% direct bookings with Mews Payments automating 60% of payments. HotelTechReport ranking: Named #1 Property Management System provider in the 2026 HotelTech Awards, with 93% of 773 surveyed hotels recommending Mews. Global hotel chain (12,500+ properties): Reported 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% RevPAR uplift, and 70% average RevPAG growth after switching to Mews. 6. Summary Table Category Examples Scenarios Front‑Desk Automation Self‑service kiosks, digital keys, automated housekeeping alerts Boutique aparthotel reduces check‑in time by 5 min, onboarding in 2 weeks Revenue Management Atomize RMS, upsell engine, direct‑booking portal Chain sees +39 % RevPAR, +70 % RevPAG, +45 % direct bookings Guest Experience Online check‑in/out, mobile payments, guest journey platform Hostel network offers contact‑free stays, reduces front‑desk calls Multi‑Property Management Single dashboard for 12,500+ properties, real‑time KPI reporting Global brand scales operations without downtime Integrations & Marketplace 800+ third‑party apps, Open API, embedded payments Hotels customise tech stack, automate 100 % of payments Sources: The Hospitality Management Software of the Future | Mews Locale customer story | Mews case study Mews Reviews & Product Details

Mews📚
Executive Summary
Value Proposition & Problem: Mews offers a cloud‑native property management system that eliminates manual, fragmented hotel operations, enabling hoteliers to focus on guest experience and profitability.  
Product / Service: A unified hospitality platform covering PMS, payments, revenue management, bookings, and marketplace integrations, with AI‑driven automation and an open API.  
Market Stage & Traction: Over 12,500 properties in 85 countries use Mews, representing a customer base growth of 85 % year‑over‑year and a transaction volume of $19.7 billion in 2025.  
Achievements: Recognized as Best PMS (2024‑2026) and Best POS (2026) by Hotel Tech Report, 45 % increase in direct bookings, 39 % rise in RevPAR, and 70 % average RevPAG uplift for customers.

Content: \\[New York, 22 January 2026]\\ – Mews , the operating system for hospitality, today announced it has raised $300 million in a Series D funding round led by EQT Growth , with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners , and participation from existing investors Kinnevik , Battery Ventures and Tiger Global . This investment marks a defining moment for hospitality technology and values the company at $2.5 billion. Following a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%, this fundraise establishes Mews as the leading hospitality tech provider for hotels of all sizes. In 2025, the company achieved: Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: - A customer base of 15,000, with 132,000+ monthly active hoteliers in 85 countries - 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk 1 - Platform Transaction Volumes of $19.7 billion - Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% - Created more than half a billion ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces 2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations “With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry,” said Matt Welle, CEO of Mews. “We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry.” \\A new standard for a global industry\\ Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... HomeFundingWith €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand out across AI platforms
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
October 16, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Omnia
EU-Startups has learned that Madrid-based Omnia, an agentic platform that helps brands boost their presence across AI engines and assistants, has raised €3.5 million in pre-Seed financing to accelerate their product roadmap, fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion.
The round was led by Visionaries Club, with participation from KFund and Baobab Ventures. Angel investors include Carles Reina (ElevenLabs), Paulo Rodriguez (Vanta) and Hugo Arévalo (ThePower).
“_AI assistants are becoming the front door to the internet_,” says Daniel Espejo, CEO and Co-founder of Omnia. “_Omnia gives teams the playbook and the agents to act on it. Moving from dashboards to done. Just like Cursor makes engineers 10× more productive, Omnia makes marketers 10× more effective on the channels that matter now_.”
Omnia’s pre-Seed round aligns with a growing wave of European investment into agentic and AI-driven marketing platforms in 2025.
Comparable early-stage rounds include Massive Dynamic (€3 million, Paris) and Tandem (€3.2 million, Paris), both developing AI-based orchestration tools. Further up the scale, Sweden’s EvoluteIQ secured €44 million to expand its enterprise agentic-AI suite, while Italy’s SylloTips (€4.2 million) and the Netherlands’ Aizy (€1.5 million) also closed funding to advance AI-based marketing and agent platforms.
Within this context, there haven’t been many comparable funding rounds in Spain, positioning Omnia as an early mover in Spain’s emerging agentic-AI marketing ecosystem.
“_As search shifts rapidly from browsers to AI engines and assistants, every brand will need an agentic strategy to stay visible,_” saidRobert Jaeckle, Partner at Visionaries Club. “_Omnia is pioneering this space by building the agentic platform that turns visibility into action for marketeers. We’re so excited to partner with Daniel and Miguel as they define this new category for Europe and beyond._”
Omnia was founded in 2025 by Daniel Espejo (CEO), formerly at Klarna where he launched markets and scaled products used by millions, and Miguel Fernández (CTO), a platform engineer with experience at GitHub and Fastly.
Omnia is an agentic platform for AI search visibility, helping brands discover high-demand topics in AI ​assistants, monitor their positioning, understand the sources those assistants cite, and launch agents to create and place AI-optimised content where it matters.
The company enables marketing and growth teams to influence how AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews answer customer questions.
The platform:
   Surfaces the most searched topics relevant to a brand.
   Benchmarks how a brand performs vs. competitors.
   Reveals the sources AI engines cite.
   Launches agents that create and place AI-optimized content in the right sources, closing the loop from insights to action.
Since launch, Omnia has been adopted by brands including Personio, ElevenLabs, Embat, Línea Directa, Ironhack, Air Europa, Praktika.ai , Trade Republic, Pleo and HappyRobot. ​
Also by marketing agencies like MIO group, Elogia, Product Hackers and Everybody. Early customers are allegedly reporting up to 3× visibility gains across AI channels.
“_Omnia has been key to our growth – helping us understand what potential clients are searching for, how well we’re positioned, and how to capture leads from AI across different markets,_” shared Adrian Varez, Head of Growth at Happy Robot.
The new funding will accelerate Omnia’s product roadmap (expanding topic intelligence, source coverage, and agentic placement), fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion from its base in Madrid, Spain.

EU-Startups has learned that Kabilio, a Barcelona-based company specialising in the application of AI to automate accounting and tax processes, has closed a €4 million pre-Seed funding round.
The round was led by international venture capital funds Visionaries Club and Picus Capital. The round also includes €200k in public funding from ENISA, reportedly making it one of the largest pre-Seed rounds in Spain.
“We have seen firsthand the challenge of attracting and retaining talent in accounting firms because the work is concentrated on repetitive tasks and peak workloads. We have also perceived a certain fear of adopting AI.
“It is precisely to change this that we created ​Kabilio, and the closing of this funding round gives us the definitive push to help these firms alleviate their teams’ workload and allow them to focus on providing more value to their clients, which is what they are truly trained for,” states Jose Ojeda, co-CEO and founder of Kabilio.
The funding round for Kabilio fits into a broader European movement of 2025 investments in AI-driven financial software.
Comparable activity includes Bluebook (Sweden), which raised €2.4 million pre-Seed to advance its self-driving accounting platform; Stacks (Netherlands), which secured €9.5 million to streamline month-end financial close processes using AI; and Plino (Italy), which obtained €650k to enhance SMEs’ financial planning and analysis.
Within this context, Kabilio’s €4 million pre-Seed is comparatively substantial, signalling investor confidence in AI solutions addressing accounting inefficiencies.
While other notable rounds have occurred in Northern and Central Europe, Kabilio’s success highlights Spain’s emerging role in this segment, marking one of the country’s largest early-stage financings in AI-powered accounting.
Robert Jäckle, Partner at Visionaries Club, highlights: “Kabilio brings AI to the heart of advisory firms not to replace accountants, but to make their work smarter and more valuable. They are not creating ‘hype’; they are solving a real operational problem. We believe Kabilio can become the leading platform for modern accounting in Europe.”
Founded in 2024 by Jose Ojeda (formerly of McKinsey, Rocket Internet, and 011h) and Álex  
Valls (formerly of Social Point, Exoticca, and 011h), the startup has developed a platform  
that uses generative AI to help accounting firms and their clients modernise the way  
they work together.
Kabilio was born from the conviction that AI will transform the advisory sector and from a commitment to support them in this transition. This mission has already earned the trust of nearly 100 accounting firms using its platform, who are seeing a productivity increase of up to 50%.
Florian Reichert, Managing Director at Picus Capital, adds that the platform “will enable advisors to collaborate more efficiently with their clients, leveraging AI to automate the most tedious tasks and generate strategic value”.
In Spain, there are approximately 65,000 accounting and tax advisory firms that work with millions of SMEs and freelancers. The current workflow presents significant inefficiencies: clients must manually collect and send information, which, due to their operational load, often happens late or incompletely.
This is exacerbated during quarterly peak periods, when advisors must concentrate a great deal of effort on checks and manual data entry within a few days.
Kabilio currently features three integrated products:
 Intelligent invoice processing: Allows receiving documents from multiple channels (email, Dropbox, etc.) and processing even the most complex invoices, such as intra-community and extra-community, reverse charge VAT, or multiple invoices in a single file. Furthermore, it suggests appropriate accounting accounts based on the content of each invoice and the company’s activity, with 97% accuracy.
 Advanced bank reconciliation: Securely connects with 99% of Spanish banks, reducing the need to request statements. The platform suggests matches between invoices and transactions using AI rules, allowing the advisor to view and edit all information in a single environment.
 Verifactu-Compliant invoicing: A modern, intuitive, and cost-effective product for SMEs and freelancers that allows for issuing customised invoices and synchronises​ all information with the advisor in real-time, eliminating the manual sending of data.
Additionally, the company is piloting its AI agent, Kabi, with several clients, which is being officially presented at Accountex Spain 2025. In its initial phase, it will allow users to query and extract information from the platform using natural language, and future versions will enable broader actions.
“We are experiencing a technological turning point similar to the arrival of software in companies two decades ago. Artificial intelligence will not only optimise processes but will also change the nature of work and the relationship between advisory firms and their clients. Our goal is to build the infrastructure that makes this new paradigm possible”, said Álex Valls, co-CEO and co-founder of Kabilio.

Spain’s HealthTech startup Tucuvi raises €17 million to scale LOLA voice AI, reporting up to 80% automation in nursing follow-up
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
Tucuvi, a Madrid-based HealthTech company innovating certified voice AI, raised €17 million ($20 million) in Series A funding to accelerate the adoption of its AI-based care management platform in healthcare systems.
The round was led by Cathay Innovation and Kfund, through its early-growth fund Leadwind, with participation from existing investors Frontline Ventures, Seaya Ventures, and Shilling.
“Healthcare is under enormous pressure, and partial solutions are no longer enough,” explains María González, CEO of Tucuvi. “To continue providing quality care at scale, healthcare systems need AI they can trust: secure, auditable, and designed for real clinical environments. We are proud to already be working with more than 60 healthcare organisations and continue to grow to meet increasing demand.” (Translated).
Tucuvi’s Series A round can be situated within a wider, still relatively selective flow of European capital into AI-driven healthcare platforms during 2025–2026.
In early 2025, Warsaw-based primary care startup Jutro Medical raised €12 million in a Series A round to expand its network of physical clinics and deploy medical AI agents across telemedicine and operational workflows. This was followed later in the year by a €24 million Series A extension, also reported by EU-Startups, to further scale its AI-first primary care model and automation capabilities across clinical and administrative functions.
Against this backdrop, Tucuvi’s Madrid-based focus on certified voice AI and end-to-end care management workflows reflects a parallel approach to addressing workforce shortages and care complexity, albeit through software deployed directly into existing healthcare systems rather than through direct care delivery.
Taken together, the roughly €53 million raised across these announcements points to a growing, though still concentrated, level of investment in European healthcare AI companies seeking to increase care capacity and operational efficiency through automation.
“The Tucuvi platform transforms protocols and clinical guidelines into real, reliable, and consistent AI workflows, allowing us to interact with all our patients and enabling our nursing team to devote their expertise to cases that require high-value clinical judgment,”  adds an unidentified Medical Director. (Translated).
Healthcare systems are facing increasing pressure from a shortage of professionals, rising demand for care, and greater operational complexity. Tucuvi was founded in 2019 to respond to this context with secure, auditable, and autonomous AI agents that aim to expand care capacity without compromising clinical quality or trust.
The company has developed an AI-powered Care Management platform designed to automate and orchestrate high-volume care workflows from start to finish.
The platform is based on AI agents, including LOLA, a voice-based AI agent that conducts secure phone conversations with patients, executes clinical and care coordination workflows, and escalates to human teams when necessary.
Tucuvi currently supports more than 50 workflows, ranging from post-surgical follow-up and discharge to chronic disease management, preoperative assessments, test preparation, screening programs, appointment scheduling, and demand management.
Tucuvi’s voice AI agents handle phone interactions, while the platform as a whole takes care of workflow logic, referrals, documentation, and integration with existing healthcare system operations.
“What convinced us at Cathay Innovation is Tucuvi’s ability to combine clinical rigor, regulatory credibility, and real-world scalability in a single platform,” said Jacky Abitbol, Managing Partner at Cathay Innovation. “Its approach allows healthcare organisations to deploy autonomous AI with confidence, freeing up significant capacity for care teams while maintaining the highest levels of safety and quality. We believe Tucuvi is blazing a new trail in how AI can be responsibly integrated into healthcare operations.”
Tucuvi is reportedly the first AI platform to obtain European regulatory approval as a Class IIb Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) for both its voice agent and patient management platform.
The company outlines that their platform has generated consistent and measurable results in collaborating healthcare systems, including:
 Up to 80% automation of nursing follow-up flows
 Patient reach and adherence greater than 90%, even in highly complex populations
 Improved outcomes in chronic diseases, such as a 5.5% reduction in readmissions for COPD
With this funding, Tucuvi will accelerate its growth strategy in Europe and the United States, expand its AI capabilities, and continue to evolve its platform as a comprehensive action and intelligence system for care teams.

What are the use cases of Mews?
1. Use Cases by Category Front‑Desk Automation: Streamlines check‑in, check‑out and housekeeping communication. Self‑service kiosks reduce check‑in time by 5 minutes. Digital key issuance eliminates front‑desk bottlenecks. Automated housekeeping notifications cut call volume. Revenue Management & Upselling: Optimizes pricing and drives ancillary sales. Dynamic pricing engine (Atomize) boosts RevPAR by 39%. Embedded upsell offers increase average RevPAG by 70%. Direct‑booking engine lifts bookings by 45%. Guest Experience Personalisation: Creates seamless, end‑to‑end journeys. Mews Guest Journey platform unifies booking, check‑in, in‑stay services. Online check‑in/out and mobile payments cut guest wait times. Smart‑device integrations (e.g., Operto) enable contact‑free stays. Multi‑Property & Chain Management: Centralises operations across dozens of locations. Single dashboard controls 12,500+ properties worldwide. Unified reporting provides real‑time KPI visibility. Marketplace of 800+ third‑party apps scales functionality. Integrations & Marketplace: Extends core PMS with specialist tools. Open API enables instant connection to accounting, AI, and distribution systems. Marketplace offers plug‑and‑play modules for loyalty, marketing, and POS. Seamless payment processing automates 100% of transactions. 2. Scenarios and Narratives Rapid On‑boarding of a Boutique Aparthotel: The property replaces a legacy system with Mews, achieving a two‑week remote deployment and 100% online onboarding. Scenario: The hotel’s small IT team uses Mews Implementation Manager’s guided rollout, enabling staff to learn the interface in days. Guests now self‑check‑in via mobile, saving five minutes per arrival, while 60% of payments are processed automatically, freeing up admin time. Chain‑wide Revenue Optimisation: A hotel group leverages the Atomize RMS to adjust rates in real time across 30 locations. Scenario: By feeding market data into the cloud‑native engine, the chain sees a 39% uplift in RevPAR and a 70% increase in average RevPAG through targeted upsells, all visible in a single analytics dashboard. Seamless Guest Journey for a Hostels Network: Hosts integrate Mews with smart‑lock and IoT platforms. Scenario: Guests book online, receive a digital key on their phone, and can request housekeeping via the virtual concierge. The integration reduces front‑desk calls and improves NPS, while the marketplace supplies a one‑click loyalty app. 3. Strategic Benefits Operational Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks cuts staff workload, shortens check‑in times by five minutes and eliminates manual billing errors. Revenue Growth: Direct‑booking engine, dynamic pricing, and automated upsells deliver double‑digit percentage lifts in bookings and RevPAR. Scalability & Agility: Cloud‑native architecture supports rapid rollout to new properties without downtime, enabling multi‑property expansion. Guest Loyalty & Satisfaction: Unified guest journey and contact‑free services increase satisfaction scores and repeat bookings. 4. Core Functionalities Property Management System (PMS): Centralised reservation, billing, and housekeeping module with real‑time status updates. Revenue Management System (RMS – Atomize): AI‑driven pricing optimisation that reacts to market demand instantly. Booking Engine & Digital Key: Integrated front‑end for direct bookings and mobile key issuance. Mews Marketplace & Open API: Over 800 pre‑built integrations and a developer‑friendly API for custom extensions. Embedded Payments: PCI‑DSS‑compliant payment processing that automates 100% of transactions. 5. Customer Success Examples Locale (US aparthotel brand): Achieved 100% online onboarding, saved five minutes per check‑in, and drove >50% direct bookings with Mews Payments automating 60% of payments. HotelTechReport ranking: Named #1 Property Management System provider in the 2026 HotelTech Awards, with 93% of 773 surveyed hotels recommending Mews. Global hotel chain (12,500+ properties): Reported 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% RevPAR uplift, and 70% average RevPAG growth after switching to Mews. 6. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content:  Amsterdam-based \\Mews\\ , an OS for hospitality, today announced it has raised €255 million ($300 million) in a Series D funding round in order to further invest in AI, embed agent-driven systems to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, improve the guest experience, and accelerate how products are built and deployed The round was led by EQT Growth, with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners, and participation from existing investors Kinnevik, Battery Ventures and Tiger Global – valuing the company at €2.1 billion ($2.5 billion). Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling\ .” In the context of recent European hospitality and HotelTech funding activity, Amsterdam-based Mews’ €255 million Series D represents one of the largest pure-play software raises in the sector. ... Also of note, is that in March 2024 Mews also raised €101 million to help hoteliers embrace cloud technology . “ \Hospitality is the business of experiences,\ ” adds Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “ \The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry.\ ” Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... FundingSpain-Startups
With €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand out across AI platforms
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
October 16, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Omnia
EU-Startups has learned that Madrid-based Omnia, an agentic platform that helps brands boost their presence across AI engines and assistants, has raised €3.5 million in pre-Seed financing to accelerate their product roadmap, fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion.
The round was led by Visionaries Club, with participation from KFund and Baobab Ventures. Angel investors include Carles Reina (ElevenLabs), Paulo Rodriguez (Vanta) and Hugo Arévalo (ThePower).
“_AI assistants are becoming the front door to the internet_,” says Daniel Espejo, CEO and Co-founder of Omnia. “_Omnia gives teams the playbook and the agents to act on it. Moving from dashboards to done. Just like Cursor makes engineers 10× more productive, Omnia makes marketers 10× more effective on the channels that matter now_.”
Omnia’s pre-Seed round aligns with a growing wave of European investment into agentic and AI-driven marketing platforms in 2025.
Comparable early-stage rounds include Massive Dynamic (€3 million, Paris) and Tandem (€3.2 million, Paris), both developing AI-based orchestration tools. Further up the scale, Sweden’s EvoluteIQ secured €44 million to expand its enterprise agentic-AI suite, while Italy’s SylloTips (€4.2 million) and the Netherlands’ Aizy (€1.5 million) also closed funding to advance AI-based marketing and agent platforms.
Within this context, there haven’t been many comparable funding rounds in Spain, positioning Omnia as an early mover in Spain’s emerging agentic-AI marketing ecosystem.
“_As search shifts rapidly from browsers to AI engines and assistants, every brand will need an agentic strategy to stay visible,_” saidRobert Jaeckle, Partner at Visionaries Club. “_Omnia is pioneering this space by building the agentic platform that turns visibility into action for marketeers. We’re so excited to partner with Daniel and Miguel as they define this new category for Europe and beyond._”
Omnia was founded in 2025 by Daniel Espejo (CEO), formerly at Klarna where he launched markets and scaled products used by millions, and Miguel Fernández (CTO), a platform engineer with experience at GitHub and Fastly.
Omnia is an agentic platform for AI search visibility, helping brands discover high-demand topics in AI ​assistants, monitor their positioning, understand the sources those assistants cite, and launch agents to create and place AI-optimised content where it matters.
The company enables marketing and growth teams to influence how AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews answer customer questions.
The platform:
   Surfaces the most searched topics relevant to a brand.
   Benchmarks how a brand performs vs. competitors.
   Reveals the sources AI engines cite.
   Launches agents that create and place AI-optimized content in the right sources, closing the loop from insights to action.
Since launch, Omnia has been adopted by brands including Personio, ElevenLabs, Embat, Línea Directa, Ironhack, Air Europa, Praktika.ai , Trade Republic, Pleo and HappyRobot. ​
Also by marketing agencies like MIO group, Elogia, Product Hackers and Everybody. Early customers are allegedly reporting up to 3× visibility gains across AI channels.
“_Omnia has been key to our growth – helping us understand what potential clients are searching for, how well we’re positioned, and how to capture leads from AI across different markets,_” shared Adrian Varez, Head of Growth at Happy Robot.
The new funding will accelerate Omnia’s product roadmap (expanding topic intelligence, source coverage, and agentic placement), fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion from its base in Madrid, Spain.

FundingSpain-Startups
With €4 million in funding, Kabilio scales AI tools helping Spanish accounting firms lift productivity by up to 50%
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
EU-Startups has learned that Kabilio, a Barcelona-based company specialising in the application of AI to automate accounting and tax processes, has closed a €4 million pre-Seed funding round.
The round was led by international venture capital funds Visionaries Club and Picus Capital. The round also includes €200k in public funding from ENISA, reportedly making it one of the largest pre-Seed rounds in Spain.
“We have seen firsthand the challenge of attracting and retaining talent in accounting firms because the work is concentrated on repetitive tasks and peak workloads. We have also perceived a certain fear of adopting AI.
“It is precisely to change this that we created ​Kabilio, and the closing of this funding round gives us the definitive push to help these firms alleviate their teams’ workload and allow them to focus on providing more value to their clients, which is what they are truly trained for,” states Jose Ojeda, co-CEO and founder of Kabilio.
The funding round for Kabilio fits into a broader European movement of 2025 investments in AI-driven financial software.
Comparable activity includes Bluebook (Sweden), which raised €2.4 million pre-Seed to advance its self-driving accounting platform; Stacks (Netherlands), which secured €9.5 million to streamline month-end financial close processes using AI; and Plino (Italy), which obtained €650k to enhance SMEs’ financial planning and analysis.
Within this context, Kabilio’s €4 million pre-Seed is comparatively substantial, signalling investor confidence in AI solutions addressing accounting inefficiencies.
While other notable rounds have occurred in Northern and Central Europe, Kabilio’s success highlights Spain’s emerging role in this segment, marking one of the country’s largest early-stage financings in AI-powered accounting.
Robert Jäckle, Partner at Visionaries Club, highlights: “Kabilio brings AI to the heart of advisory firms not to replace accountants, but to make their work smarter and more valuable. They are not creating ‘hype’; they are solving a real operational problem. We believe Kabilio can become the leading platform for modern accounting in Europe.”
Founded in 2024 by Jose Ojeda (formerly of McKinsey, Rocket Internet, and 011h) and Álex  
Valls (formerly of Social Point, Exoticca, and 011h), the startup has developed a platform  
that uses generative AI to help accounting firms and their clients modernise the way  
they work together.
Kabilio was born from the conviction that AI will transform the advisory sector and from a commitment to support them in this transition. This mission has already earned the trust of nearly 100 accounting firms using its platform, who are seeing a productivity increase of up to 50%.
Florian Reichert, Managing Director at Picus Capital, adds that the platform “will enable advisors to collaborate more efficiently with their clients, leveraging AI to automate the most tedious tasks and generate strategic value”.
In Spain, there are approximately 65,000 accounting and tax advisory firms that work with millions of SMEs and freelancers. The current workflow presents significant inefficiencies: clients must manually collect and send information, which, due to their operational load, often happens late or incompletely.
This is exacerbated during quarterly peak periods, when advisors must concentrate a great deal of effort on checks and manual data entry within a few days.
Kabilio currently features three integrated products:
 Intelligent invoice processing: Allows receiving documents from multiple channels (email, Dropbox, etc.) and processing even the most complex invoices, such as intra-community and extra-community, reverse charge VAT, or multiple invoices in a single file. Furthermore, it suggests appropriate accounting accounts based on the content of each invoice and the company’s activity, with 97% accuracy.
 Advanced bank reconciliation: Securely connects with 99% of Spanish banks, reducing the need to request statements. The platform suggests matches between invoices and transactions using AI rules, allowing the advisor to view and edit all information in a single environment.
 Verifactu-Compliant invoicing: A modern, intuitive, and cost-effective product for SMEs and freelancers that allows for issuing customised invoices and synchronises​ all information with the advisor in real-time, eliminating the manual sending of data.
Additionally, the company is piloting its AI agent, Kabi, with several clients, which is being officially presented at Accountex Spain 2025. In its initial phase, it will allow users to query and extract information from the platform using natural language, and future versions will enable broader actions.
“We are experiencing a technological turning point similar to the arrival of software in companies two decades ago. Artificial intelligence will not only optimise processes but will also change the nature of work and the relationship between advisory firms and their clients. Our goal is to build the infrastructure that makes this new paradigm possible”, said Álex Valls, co-CEO and co-founder of Kabilio.

HomeFundingSpain's HealthTech startup Tucuvi raises €17 million to scale LOLA voice AI,...
FundingSpain-Startups
Spain’s HealthTech startup Tucuvi raises €17 million to scale LOLA voice AI, reporting up to 80% automation in nursing follow-up
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
Tucuvi, a Madrid-based HealthTech company innovating certified voice AI, raised €17 million ($20 million) in Series A funding to accelerate the adoption of its AI-based care management platform in healthcare systems.
The round was led by Cathay Innovation and Kfund, through its early-growth fund Leadwind, with participation from existing investors Frontline Ventures, Seaya Ventures, and Shilling.
“Healthcare is under enormous pressure, and partial solutions are no longer enough,” explains María González, CEO of Tucuvi. “To continue providing quality care at scale, healthcare systems need AI they can trust: secure, auditable, and designed for real clinical environments. We are proud to already be working with more than 60 healthcare organisations and continue to grow to meet increasing demand.” (Translated).
Tucuvi’s Series A round can be situated within a wider, still relatively selective flow of European capital into AI-driven healthcare platforms during 2025–2026.
In early 2025, Warsaw-based primary care startup Jutro Medical raised €12 million in a Series A round to expand its network of physical clinics and deploy medical AI agents across telemedicine and operational workflows. This was followed later in the year by a €24 million Series A extension, also reported by EU-Startups, to further scale its AI-first primary care model and automation capabilities across clinical and administrative functions.
Against this backdrop, Tucuvi’s Madrid-based focus on certified voice AI and end-to-end care management workflows reflects a parallel approach to addressing workforce shortages and care complexity, albeit through software deployed directly into existing healthcare systems rather than through direct care delivery.
Taken together, the roughly €53 million raised across these announcements points to a growing, though still concentrated, level of investment in European healthcare AI companies seeking to increase care capacity and operational efficiency through automation.
“The Tucuvi platform transforms protocols and clinical guidelines into real, reliable, and consistent AI workflows, allowing us to interact with all our patients and enabling our nursing team to devote their expertise to cases that require high-value clinical judgment,”  adds an unidentified Medical Director. (Translated).
Healthcare systems are facing increasing pressure from a shortage of professionals, rising demand for care, and greater operational complexity. Tucuvi was founded in 2019 to respond to this context with secure, auditable, and autonomous AI agents that aim to expand care capacity without compromising clinical quality or trust.
The company has developed an AI-powered Care Management platform designed to automate and orchestrate high-volume care workflows from start to finish.
The platform is based on AI agents, including LOLA, a voice-based AI agent that conducts secure phone conversations with patients, executes clinical and care coordination workflows, and escalates to human teams when necessary.
Tucuvi currently supports more than 50 workflows, ranging from post-surgical follow-up and discharge to chronic disease management, preoperative assessments, test preparation, screening programs, appointment scheduling, and demand management.
Tucuvi’s voice AI agents handle phone interactions, while the platform as a whole takes care of workflow logic, referrals, documentation, and integration with existing healthcare system operations.
“What convinced us at Cathay Innovation is Tucuvi’s ability to combine clinical rigor, regulatory credibility, and real-world scalability in a single platform,” said Jacky Abitbol, Managing Partner at Cathay Innovation. “Its approach allows healthcare organisations to deploy autonomous AI with confidence, freeing up significant capacity for care teams while maintaining the highest levels of safety and quality. We believe Tucuvi is blazing a new trail in how AI can be responsibly integrated into healthcare operations.”
Tucuvi is reportedly the first AI platform to obtain European regulatory approval as a Class IIb Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) for both its voice agent and patient management platform.
The company outlines that their platform has generated consistent and measurable results in collaborating healthcare systems, including:
 Up to 80% automation of nursing follow-up flows
 Patient reach and adherence greater than 90%, even in highly complex populations
 Improved outcomes in chronic diseases, such as a 5.5% reduction in readmissions for COPD
With this funding, Tucuvi will accelerate its growth strategy in Europe and the United States, expand its AI capabilities, and continue to evolve its platform as a comprehensive action and intelligence system for care teams.

Mews is a hospitality technology company providing an operating system for hotels, offering property management, payments, and integrations to modernize the hospitality industry.

What are the use cases of Mews?
1. Use Cases by Category Front‑Desk Automation: Streamlines check‑in, check‑out and housekeeping communication. Self‑service kiosks reduce check‑in time by 5 minutes. Digital key issuance eliminates front‑desk bottlenecks. Automated housekeeping notifications cut call volume. Revenue Management & Upselling: Optimizes pricing and drives ancillary sales. Dynamic pricing engine (Atomize) boosts RevPAR by 39%. Embedded upsell offers increase average RevPAG by 70%. Direct‑booking engine lifts bookings by 45%. Guest Experience Personalisation: Creates seamless, end‑to‑end journeys. Mews Guest Journey platform unifies booking, check‑in, in‑stay services. Online check‑in/out and mobile payments cut guest wait times. Smart‑device integrations (e.g., Operto) enable contact‑free stays. Multi‑Property & Chain Management: Centralises operations across dozens of locations. Single dashboard controls 12,500+ properties worldwide. Unified reporting provides real‑time KPI visibility. Marketplace of 800+ third‑party apps scales functionality. Integrations & Marketplace: Extends core PMS with specialist tools. Open API enables instant connection to accounting, AI, and distribution systems. Marketplace offers plug‑and‑play modules for loyalty, marketing, and POS. Seamless payment processing automates 100% of transactions. 2. Scenarios and Narratives Rapid On‑boarding of a Boutique Aparthotel: The property replaces a legacy system with Mews, achieving a two‑week remote deployment and 100% online onboarding. Scenario: The hotel’s small IT team uses Mews Implementation Manager’s guided rollout, enabling staff to learn the interface in days. Guests now self‑check‑in via mobile, saving five minutes per arrival, while 60% of payments are processed automatically, freeing up admin time. Chain‑wide Revenue Optimisation: A hotel group leverages the Atomize RMS to adjust rates in real time across 30 locations. Scenario: By feeding market data into the cloud‑native engine, the chain sees a 39% uplift in RevPAR and a 70% increase in average RevPAG through targeted upsells, all visible in a single analytics dashboard. Seamless Guest Journey for a Hostels Network: Hosts integrate Mews with smart‑lock and IoT platforms. Scenario: Guests book online, receive a digital key on their phone, and can request housekeeping via the virtual concierge. The integration reduces front‑desk calls and improves NPS, while the marketplace supplies a one‑click loyalty app. 3. Strategic Benefits Operational Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks cuts staff workload, shortens check‑in times by five minutes and eliminates manual billing errors. Revenue Growth: Direct‑booking engine, dynamic pricing, and automated upsells deliver double‑digit percentage lifts in bookings and RevPAR. Scalability & Agility: Cloud‑native architecture supports rapid rollout to new properties without downtime, enabling multi‑property expansion. Guest Loyalty & Satisfaction: Unified guest journey and contact‑free services increase satisfaction scores and repeat bookings. 4. Core Functionalities Property Management System (PMS): Centralised reservation, billing, and housekeeping module with real‑time status updates. Revenue Management System (RMS – Atomize): AI‑driven pricing optimisation that reacts to market demand instantly. Booking Engine & Digital Key: Integrated front‑end for direct bookings and mobile key issuance. Mews Marketplace & Open API: Over 800 pre‑built integrations and a developer‑friendly API for custom extensions. Embedded Payments: PCI‑DSS‑compliant payment processing that automates 100% of transactions. 5. Customer Success Examples Locale (US aparthotel brand): Achieved 100% online onboarding, saved five minutes per check‑in, and drove >50% direct bookings with Mews Payments automating 60% of payments. HotelTechReport ranking: Named #1 Property Management System provider in the 2026 HotelTech Awards, with 93% of 773 surveyed hotels recommending Mews. Global hotel chain (12,500+ properties): Reported 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% RevPAR uplift, and 70% average RevPAG growth after switching to Mews. 6. Executive Summary
Value Proposition & Problem: Mews offers a cloud‑native property management system that eliminates manual, fragmented hotel operations, enabling hoteliers to focus on guest experience and profitability.  
Product / Service: A unified hospitality platform covering PMS, payments, revenue management, bookings, and marketplace integrations, with AI‑driven automation and an open API.  
Market Stage & Traction: Over 12,500 properties in 85 countries use Mews, representing a customer base growth of 85 % year‑over‑year and a transaction volume of $19.7 billion in 2025.  
Achievements: Recognized as Best PMS (2024‑2026) and Best POS (2026) by Hotel Tech Report, 45 % increase in direct bookings, 39 % rise in RevPAR, and 70 % average RevPAG uplift for customers.

Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Published January 22, 2026 Press \EQT Growth leads Series D funding round to accelerate the AI-native future of hotels globally, with participation from new investors Atomico and HarbourVest\ \Investment values Mews at $2.5 billion\ \Mews’ Platform Transaction Volume reached $19.7 billion in 2025\ Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: \\[New York, 22 January 2026]\\ – Mews , the operating system for hospitality, today announced it has raised $300 million in a Series D funding round led by EQT Growth , with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners , and participation from existing investors Kinnevik , Battery Ventures and Tiger Global . This investment marks a defining moment for hospitality technology and values the company at $2.5 billion. Following a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%, this fundraise establishes Mews as the leading hospitality tech provider for hotels of all sizes. In 2025, the company achieved: Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: - A customer base of 15,000, with 132,000+ monthly active hoteliers in 85 countries - 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk 1 - Platform Transaction Volumes of $19.7 billion - Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% - Created more than half a billion ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces 2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations “With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry,” said Matt Welle, CEO of Mews. “We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: The funding will expand Mews’ investments in artificial intelligence, embedding agent-driven systems across the platform to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, significantly improve the guest experience and accelerate how products are built and deployed - enabling properties to: - Orchestrate operations in real time with intelligent automation - Optimize revenue and profit with advanced insights into guest behavior - Scale business without the debt of legacy tech - Radically simplify workflows to onboard teams faster Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry.” \\A new standard for a global industry\\ Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: “I’ve had the pleasure of getting to know the Mews team for several years and witness the company go from a bold vision to an organization delivering at scale,” said Kirk Lepke, Partner at EQT Growth. “Hospitality is one of the world’s largest industries, yet its core systems remain decades behind. Mews is creating a modern technology standard, an AI-enabled hospitality operating system that helps solve the fragmentation we see in the industry. We at EQT Growth are incredibly excited to lead this round and partner with Mews.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content:  Amsterdam-based \\Mews\\ , an OS for hospitality, today announced it has raised €255 million ($300 million) in a Series D funding round in order to further invest in AI, embed agent-driven systems to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, improve the guest experience, and accelerate how products are built and deployed The round was led by EQT Growth, with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners, and participation from existing investors Kinnevik, Battery Ventures and Tiger Global – valuing the company at €2.1 billion ($2.5 billion). Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling\ .” In the context of recent European hospitality and HotelTech funding activity, Amsterdam-based Mews’ €255 million Series D represents one of the largest pure-play software raises in the sector. ... Also of note, is that in March 2024 Mews also raised €101 million to help hoteliers embrace cloud technology . “ \Hospitality is the business of experiences,\ ” adds Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “ \The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. HomeFundingWith €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand out across AI platforms
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
October 16, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Omnia
EU-Startups has learned that Madrid-based Omnia, an agentic platform that helps brands boost their presence across AI engines and assistants, has raised €3.5 million in pre-Seed financing to accelerate their product roadmap, fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion.
The round was led by Visionaries Club, with participation from KFund and Baobab Ventures. Angel investors include Carles Reina (ElevenLabs), Paulo Rodriguez (Vanta) and Hugo Arévalo (ThePower).
“_AI assistants are becoming the front door to the internet_,” says Daniel Espejo, CEO and Co-founder of Omnia. “_Omnia gives teams the playbook and the agents to act on it. Moving from dashboards to done. Just like Cursor makes engineers 10× more productive, Omnia makes marketers 10× more effective on the channels that matter now_.”
Omnia’s pre-Seed round aligns with a growing wave of European investment into agentic and AI-driven marketing platforms in 2025.
Comparable early-stage rounds include Massive Dynamic (€3 million, Paris) and Tandem (€3.2 million, Paris), both developing AI-based orchestration tools. Further up the scale, Sweden’s EvoluteIQ secured €44 million to expand its enterprise agentic-AI suite, while Italy’s SylloTips (€4.2 million) and the Netherlands’ Aizy (€1.5 million) also closed funding to advance AI-based marketing and agent platforms.
Within this context, there haven’t been many comparable funding rounds in Spain, positioning Omnia as an early mover in Spain’s emerging agentic-AI marketing ecosystem.
“_As search shifts rapidly from browsers to AI engines and assistants, every brand will need an agentic strategy to stay visible,_” saidRobert Jaeckle, Partner at Visionaries Club. “_Omnia is pioneering this space by building the agentic platform that turns visibility into action for marketeers. We’re so excited to partner with Daniel and Miguel as they define this new category for Europe and beyond._”
Omnia was founded in 2025 by Daniel Espejo (CEO), formerly at Klarna where he launched markets and scaled products used by millions, and Miguel Fernández (CTO), a platform engineer with experience at GitHub and Fastly.
Omnia is an agentic platform for AI search visibility, helping brands discover high-demand topics in AI ​assistants, monitor their positioning, understand the sources those assistants cite, and launch agents to create and place AI-optimised content where it matters.
The company enables marketing and growth teams to influence how AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews answer customer questions.
The platform:
   Surfaces the most searched topics relevant to a brand.
   Benchmarks how a brand performs vs. competitors.
   Reveals the sources AI engines cite.
   Launches agents that create and place AI-optimized content in the right sources, closing the loop from insights to action.
Since launch, Omnia has been adopted by brands including Personio, ElevenLabs, Embat, Línea Directa, Ironhack, Air Europa, Praktika.ai , Trade Republic, Pleo and HappyRobot. ​
Also by marketing agencies like MIO group, Elogia, Product Hackers and Everybody. Early customers are allegedly reporting up to 3× visibility gains across AI channels.
“_Omnia has been key to our growth – helping us understand what potential clients are searching for, how well we’re positioned, and how to capture leads from AI across different markets,_” shared Adrian Varez, Head of Growth at Happy Robot.
The new funding will accelerate Omnia’s product roadmap (expanding topic intelligence, source coverage, and agentic placement), fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion from its base in Madrid, Spain.

HomeFundingWith €4 million in funding, Kabilio scales AI tools helping Spanish accounting...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €4 million in funding, Kabilio scales AI tools helping Spanish accounting firms lift productivity by up to 50%
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
EU-Startups has learned that Kabilio, a Barcelona-based company specialising in the application of AI to automate accounting and tax processes, has closed a €4 million pre-Seed funding round.
The round was led by international venture capital funds Visionaries Club and Picus Capital. The round also includes €200k in public funding from ENISA, reportedly making it one of the largest pre-Seed rounds in Spain.
“We have seen firsthand the challenge of attracting and retaining talent in accounting firms because the work is concentrated on repetitive tasks and peak workloads. We have also perceived a certain fear of adopting AI.
“It is precisely to change this that we created ​Kabilio, and the closing of this funding round gives us the definitive push to help these firms alleviate their teams’ workload and allow them to focus on providing more value to their clients, which is what they are truly trained for,” states Jose Ojeda, co-CEO and founder of Kabilio.
The funding round for Kabilio fits into a broader European movement of 2025 investments in AI-driven financial software.
Comparable activity includes Bluebook (Sweden), which raised €2.4 million pre-Seed to advance its self-driving accounting platform; Stacks (Netherlands), which secured €9.5 million to streamline month-end financial close processes using AI; and Plino (Italy), which obtained €650k to enhance SMEs’ financial planning and analysis.
Within this context, Kabilio’s €4 million pre-Seed is comparatively substantial, signalling investor confidence in AI solutions addressing accounting inefficiencies.
While other notable rounds have occurred in Northern and Central Europe, Kabilio’s success highlights Spain’s emerging role in this segment, marking one of the country’s largest early-stage financings in AI-powered accounting.
Robert Jäckle, Partner at Visionaries Club, highlights: “Kabilio brings AI to the heart of advisory firms not to replace accountants, but to make their work smarter and more valuable. They are not creating ‘hype’; they are solving a real operational problem. We believe Kabilio can become the leading platform for modern accounting in Europe.”
Founded in 2024 by Jose Ojeda (formerly of McKinsey, Rocket Internet, and 011h) and Álex  
Valls (formerly of Social Point, Exoticca, and 011h), the startup has developed a platform  
that uses generative AI to help accounting firms and their clients modernise the way  
they work together.
Kabilio was born from the conviction that AI will transform the advisory sector and from a commitment to support them in this transition. This mission has already earned the trust of nearly 100 accounting firms using its platform, who are seeing a productivity increase of up to 50%.
Florian Reichert, Managing Director at Picus Capital, adds that the platform “will enable advisors to collaborate more efficiently with their clients, leveraging AI to automate the most tedious tasks and generate strategic value”.
In Spain, there are approximately 65,000 accounting and tax advisory firms that work with millions of SMEs and freelancers. The current workflow presents significant inefficiencies: clients must manually collect and send information, which, due to their operational load, often happens late or incompletely.
This is exacerbated during quarterly peak periods, when advisors must concentrate a great deal of effort on checks and manual data entry within a few days.
Kabilio currently features three integrated products:
 Intelligent invoice processing: Allows receiving documents from multiple channels (email, Dropbox, etc.) and processing even the most complex invoices, such as intra-community and extra-community, reverse charge VAT, or multiple invoices in a single file. Furthermore, it suggests appropriate accounting accounts based on the content of each invoice and the company’s activity, with 97% accuracy.
 Advanced bank reconciliation: Securely connects with 99% of Spanish banks, reducing the need to request statements. The platform suggests matches between invoices and transactions using AI rules, allowing the advisor to view and edit all information in a single environment.
 Verifactu-Compliant invoicing: A modern, intuitive, and cost-effective product for SMEs and freelancers that allows for issuing customised invoices and synchronises​ all information with the advisor in real-time, eliminating the manual sending of data.
Additionally, the company is piloting its AI agent, Kabi, with several clients, which is being officially presented at Accountex Spain 2025. In its initial phase, it will allow users to query and extract information from the platform using natural language, and future versions will enable broader actions.
“We are experiencing a technological turning point similar to the arrival of software in companies two decades ago. Artificial intelligence will not only optimise processes but will also change the nature of work and the relationship between advisory firms and their clients. Our goal is to build the infrastructure that makes this new paradigm possible”, said Álex Valls, co-CEO and co-founder of Kabilio.

Spain’s HealthTech startup Tucuvi raises €17 million to scale LOLA voice AI, reporting up to 80% automation in nursing follow-up
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
Tucuvi, a Madrid-based HealthTech company innovating certified voice AI, raised €17 million ($20 million) in Series A funding to accelerate the adoption of its AI-based care management platform in healthcare systems.
The round was led by Cathay Innovation and Kfund, through its early-growth fund Leadwind, with participation from existing investors Frontline Ventures, Seaya Ventures, and Shilling.
“Healthcare is under enormous pressure, and partial solutions are no longer enough,” explains María González, CEO of Tucuvi. “To continue providing quality care at scale, healthcare systems need AI they can trust: secure, auditable, and designed for real clinical environments. We are proud to already be working with more than 60 healthcare organisations and continue to grow to meet increasing demand.” (Translated).
Tucuvi’s Series A round can be situated within a wider, still relatively selective flow of European capital into AI-driven healthcare platforms during 2025–2026.
In early 2025, Warsaw-based primary care startup Jutro Medical raised €12 million in a Series A round to expand its network of physical clinics and deploy medical AI agents across telemedicine and operational workflows. This was followed later in the year by a €24 million Series A extension, also reported by EU-Startups, to further scale its AI-first primary care model and automation capabilities across clinical and administrative functions.
Against this backdrop, Tucuvi’s Madrid-based focus on certified voice AI and end-to-end care management workflows reflects a parallel approach to addressing workforce shortages and care complexity, albeit through software deployed directly into existing healthcare systems rather than through direct care delivery.
Taken together, the roughly €53 million raised across these announcements points to a growing, though still concentrated, level of investment in European healthcare AI companies seeking to increase care capacity and operational efficiency through automation.
“The Tucuvi platform transforms protocols and clinical guidelines into real, reliable, and consistent AI workflows, allowing us to interact with all our patients and enabling our nursing team to devote their expertise to cases that require high-value clinical judgment,”  adds an unidentified Medical Director. (Translated).
Healthcare systems are facing increasing pressure from a shortage of professionals, rising demand for care, and greater operational complexity. Tucuvi was founded in 2019 to respond to this context with secure, auditable, and autonomous AI agents that aim to expand care capacity without compromising clinical quality or trust.
The company has developed an AI-powered Care Management platform designed to automate and orchestrate high-volume care workflows from start to finish.
The platform is based on AI agents, including LOLA, a voice-based AI agent that conducts secure phone conversations with patients, executes clinical and care coordination workflows, and escalates to human teams when necessary.
Tucuvi currently supports more than 50 workflows, ranging from post-surgical follow-up and discharge to chronic disease management, preoperative assessments, test preparation, screening programs, appointment scheduling, and demand management.
Tucuvi’s voice AI agents handle phone interactions, while the platform as a whole takes care of workflow logic, referrals, documentation, and integration with existing healthcare system operations.
“What convinced us at Cathay Innovation is Tucuvi’s ability to combine clinical rigor, regulatory credibility, and real-world scalability in a single platform,” said Jacky Abitbol, Managing Partner at Cathay Innovation. “Its approach allows healthcare organisations to deploy autonomous AI with confidence, freeing up significant capacity for care teams while maintaining the highest levels of safety and quality. We believe Tucuvi is blazing a new trail in how AI can be responsibly integrated into healthcare operations.”
Tucuvi is reportedly the first AI platform to obtain European regulatory approval as a Class IIb Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) for both its voice agent and patient management platform.
The company outlines that their platform has generated consistent and measurable results in collaborating healthcare systems, including:
 Up to 80% automation of nursing follow-up flows
 Patient reach and adherence greater than 90%, even in highly complex populations
 Improved outcomes in chronic diseases, such as a 5.5% reduction in readmissions for COPD
With this funding, Tucuvi will accelerate its growth strategy in Europe and the United States, expand its AI capabilities, and continue to evolve its platform as a comprehensive action and intelligence system for care teams.

Frequently Asked Questions about Mews

What are the use cases of Mews?
1. Use Cases by Category Front‑Desk Automation: Streamlines check‑in, check‑out and housekeeping communication. Self‑service kiosks reduce check‑in time by 5 minutes. Digital key issuance eliminates front‑desk bottlenecks. Automated housekeeping notifications cut call volume. Revenue Management & Upselling: Optimizes pricing and drives ancillary sales. Dynamic pricing engine (Atomize) boosts RevPAR by 39%. Embedded upsell offers increase average RevPAG by 70%. Direct‑booking engine lifts bookings by 45%. Guest Experience Personalisation: Creates seamless, end‑to‑end journeys. Mews Guest Journey platform unifies booking, check‑in, in‑stay services. Online check‑in/out and mobile payments cut guest wait times. Smart‑device integrations (e.g., Operto) enable contact‑free stays. Multi‑Property & Chain Management: Centralises operations across dozens of locations. Single dashboard controls 12,500+ properties worldwide. Unified reporting provides real‑time KPI visibility. Marketplace of 800+ third‑party apps scales functionality. Integrations & Marketplace: Extends core PMS with specialist tools. Open API enables instant connection to accounting, AI, and distribution systems. Marketplace offers plug‑and‑play modules for loyalty, marketing, and POS. Seamless payment processing automates 100% of transactions. 2. Scenarios and Narratives Rapid On‑boarding of a Boutique Aparthotel: The property replaces a legacy system with Mews, achieving a two‑week remote deployment and 100% online onboarding. Scenario: The hotel’s small IT team uses Mews Implementation Manager’s guided rollout, enabling staff to learn the interface in days. Guests now self‑check‑in via mobile, saving five minutes per arrival, while 60% of payments are processed automatically, freeing up admin time. Chain‑wide Revenue Optimisation: A hotel group leverages the Atomize RMS to adjust rates in real time across 30 locations. Scenario: By feeding market data into the cloud‑native engine, the chain sees a 39% uplift in RevPAR and a 70% increase in average RevPAG through targeted upsells, all visible in a single analytics dashboard. Seamless Guest Journey for a Hostels Network: Hosts integrate Mews with smart‑lock and IoT platforms. Scenario: Guests book online, receive a digital key on their phone, and can request housekeeping via the virtual concierge. The integration reduces front‑desk calls and improves NPS, while the marketplace supplies a one‑click loyalty app. 3. Strategic Benefits Operational Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks cuts staff workload, shortens check‑in times by five minutes and eliminates manual billing errors. Revenue Growth: Direct‑booking engine, dynamic pricing, and automated upsells deliver double‑digit percentage lifts in bookings and RevPAR. Scalability & Agility: Cloud‑native architecture supports rapid rollout to new properties without downtime, enabling multi‑property expansion. Guest Loyalty & Satisfaction: Unified guest journey and contact‑free services increase satisfaction scores and repeat bookings. 4. Core Functionalities Property Management System (PMS): Centralised reservation, billing, and housekeeping module with real‑time status updates. Revenue Management System (RMS – Atomize): AI‑driven pricing optimisation that reacts to market demand instantly. Booking Engine & Digital Key: Integrated front‑end for direct bookings and mobile key issuance. Mews Marketplace & Open API: Over 800 pre‑built integrations and a developer‑friendly API for custom extensions. Embedded Payments: PCI‑DSS‑compliant payment processing that automates 100% of transactions. 5. Customer Success Examples Locale (US aparthotel brand): Achieved 100% online onboarding, saved five minutes per check‑in, and drove >50% direct bookings with Mews Payments automating 60% of payments. HotelTechReport ranking: Named #1 Property Management System provider in the 2026 HotelTech Awards, with 93% of 773 surveyed hotels recommending Mews. Global hotel chain (12,500+ properties): Reported 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% RevPAR uplift, and 70% average RevPAG growth after switching to Mews. 6. Summary Table Category Examples Scenarios Front‑Desk Automation Self‑service kiosks, digital keys, automated housekeeping alerts Boutique aparthotel reduces check‑in time by 5 min, onboarding in 2 weeks Revenue Management Atomize RMS, upsell engine, direct‑booking portal Chain sees +39 % RevPAR, +70 % RevPAG, +45 % direct bookings Guest Experience Online check‑in/out, mobile payments, guest journey platform Hostel network offers contact‑free stays, reduces front‑desk calls Multi‑Property Management Single dashboard for 12,500+ properties, real‑time KPI reporting Global brand scales operations without downtime Integrations & Marketplace 800+ third‑party apps, Open API, embedded payments Hotels customise tech stack, automate 100 % of payments Sources: The Hospitality Management Software of the Future | Mews Locale customer story | Mews case study Mews Reviews & Product Details

Executive Summary
Value Proposition & Problem: Mews offers a cloud‑native property management system that eliminates manual, fragmented hotel operations, enabling hoteliers to focus on guest experience and profitability.  
Product / Service: A unified hospitality platform covering PMS, payments, revenue management, bookings, and marketplace integrations, with AI‑driven automation and an open API.  
Market Stage & Traction: Over 12,500 properties in 85 countries use Mews, representing a customer base growth of 85 % year‑over‑year and a transaction volume of $19.7 billion in 2025.  
Achievements: Recognized as Best PMS (2024‑2026) and Best POS (2026) by Hotel Tech Report, 45 % increase in direct bookings, 39 % rise in RevPAR, and 70 % average RevPAG uplift for customers.
